title: lomanthus putcalensis
side_effects: Lomanthus putcalensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and redness, particularly when consumed in large quantities or without proper preparation. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or roots may also lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high content of toxic compounds. Furthermore, improper use of Lomanthus putcalensis can trigger cardiovascular problems, such as rapid heartbeat, palpitations, and blood pressure fluctuations, in susceptible individuals. In severe cases, excessive consumption may even result in renal failure or other systemic complications, underscoring the need for caution and professional guidance.
